title: FairTax Act of 2025
plain_summary: The FairTax Act of 2025 would get rid of all federal income taxes, payroll taxes, and estate taxes starting in 2027. Instead, the government would collect money through a 23% national sales tax on things people buy. Families at or below the poverty level would get monthly rebate checks to help pay for the extra sales tax costs. States would collect this federal sales tax along with their own state taxes. The bill would also shut down the IRS after 2029 and create new tax agencies, but the whole plan would end if the constitutional amendment allowing income taxes is not repealed within seven years.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.
plain_keywords: replace income tax with sales tax

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title; Table of Contents
sections.text: This law can be called the FairTax Act of two thousand twenty-five. The law has several main parts. Title One gets rid of income taxes, payroll taxes, and estate and gift taxes. Title Two creates a new national sales tax. Title Three covers other matters like phasing out the old tax system. Title Four says the sales tax will end if the Sixteenth Amendment to the Constitution is not repealed within seven years.

sections.title: Section 2  Congressional Findings
sections.text: Congress believes the current federal income tax system has many problems. It slows down economic growth and reduces the standard of living for Americans. It makes American businesses less competitive with companies in other countries. It discourages people from saving and investing money because it taxes income multiple times. It makes it harder for businesses to grow and create jobs. The tax system is too complicated and costs too much for people and businesses to follow the rules. It is unfair and violates people's privacy rights. It hides the true cost of government by building taxes into the price of everything Americans buy. Many people do not pay what they owe, which increases the tax burden on honest taxpayers. It makes it harder for people to move up economically. Congress also finds that Social Security and Medicare payroll taxes make it more expensive to hire workers and destroy jobs. They hurt lower income Americans the most. Estate and gift taxes force families to sell their businesses and farms to pay taxes. They discourage people from starting businesses and help big companies stay dominant over small family businesses. Congress believes a national sales tax on goods and services bought for final use would be better. It would be similar to sales taxes that forty-five states already have. It would encourage saving and investment, be more fair, promote economic growth, raise the standard of living, increase investment, make American businesses more competitive, reduce the burden on taxpayers, improve economic mobility, and respect people's privacy rights. Congress finds that states have the most experience running sales tax systems, so it makes sense to let states help collect and manage the federal sales tax for a reasonable fee. Finally, Congress believes the Sixteenth Amendment to the Constitution should be repealed.

sections.title: Section 101  Income Taxes Repealed
sections.text: The part of the tax code that deals with income taxes and self-employment taxes is completely eliminated.

sections.title: Section 102  Payroll Taxes Repealed
sections.text: The part of the tax code that deals with payroll taxes and withholding income taxes from paychecks is completely eliminated. Social Security will be funded from general government revenue instead of payroll taxes.

sections.title: Section 103  Estate and Gift Taxes Repealed
sections.text: The part of the tax code that deals with estate and gift taxes is completely eliminated.

sections.title: Section 104  Conforming Amendments; Effective Date
sections.text: The tax code is reorganized to remove sections that are no longer needed. The Internal Revenue Code of nineteen eighty-six will be renamed the Internal Revenue Code of two thousand twenty-five. All references to the old code will include references to the new code and vice versa. These changes take effect on January first, two thousand twenty-seven, unless otherwise specified.

sections.title: Section 201  Sales Tax
sections.text: A new sales tax system is added to the tax code. The system has detailed rules about how to interpret the law, definitions of key terms, how the tax is imposed, credits and refunds, family consumption allowances (rebates to help families), cooperation between federal and state governments in collecting the tax, administrative provisions, collections and appeals, special rules for certain situations, financial services, and other matters. The main goal is to tax all consumption of goods and services in the United States once, but only once, while preventing double taxation and simplifying the tax system. The system is designed to respect privacy and individual rights, increase the role of state governments in federal tax collection, and promote cooperation between federal and state tax administrators. The sales tax rate starts at twenty-three percent of the total payment including tax in two thousand twenty-seven. For later years, the rate will be set to raise the same amount of money needed for general government operations, Social Security, and Medicare. The tax applies to most goods and services used or consumed in the United States, but excludes certain business purchases, exports, and government functions. Businesses and individuals can claim various credits and refunds in specific situations. Families can register to receive monthly rebate payments to offset the sales tax burden up to the poverty level. States can choose to collect and administer the federal sales tax in cooperation with their own state sales taxes, earning an administrative fee for this service.
